title: Spot and cut fake antithesis in a paragraph
guidance: Reward keeping only the earned contrast. Penalise keeping slop or stripping the legitimate one.

runner_input.instruction: Rewrite, removing manufactured 'not X, it's Y' constructions; keep only contrasts where Y is real and nameable.
runner_input.constraints: Exactly one contrast should survive. Note which and why.

source_blocks.text: Halden isn't a software company — it's a movement. This isn't about schedules, it's about trust. The moat isn't technical, it's institutional. And success here isn't about features, it's about belief.

rubric_notes: The one earned contrast is 'the moat isn't technical, it's institutional' — Y ('technical') is a real default. The other three are manufactured (movement/trust/belief have no nameable Y).
Survivor count must be exactly one.
